title: Fix the Maximum Consecutive Sublist Sum

statement.markdown: Write a function `maxConsecutiveSum` that takes an array of integers as input and returns the maximum sum of a sublist within the array. The sublist can be of any length, but the sum must be consecutive. For example, given the array `[1, -2, 3, 4, -5, 6, -7]`, the maximum sum would be `6` because the sublist `[-5, 6]` has the maximum consecutive sum. Assume that the input array is not empty and all elements are integers.
statement.outputFormat: The maximum sum of a sublist within the array
statement.notes: The input array is not empty and all elements are integers.

constraints.description: The length of the input array

examples.explanation: The sublist [-5, 6] has the maximum consecutive sum

hints.text: Use a dynamic programming approach to solve this problem
